scenario: Discourse work session - audit the Upload contract before a teardown rework

map_quality.rationale: The answer names the project, directory layout, Upload's exact location (app/models/upload.rb:5 with key schema columns), and all primary supporting pieces (HasUrl, UploadReference, file store, OptimizedImage) — the agent can pick an entry point and act with small lookups. The email/backup/theming areas are hinted via lib/ but not surfaced as directories.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every piece is anchored by file:line — app/models/concerns/has_url.rb:3, app/models/upload_reference.rb:3, lib/file_store/base_store.rb:7, app/models/optimized_image.rb:3 — giving the agent exact targets with no vagueness.

justification.rationale: HasUrl is explained as 'URL→record resolution by sha1/url', UploadReference as 'polymorphic glue', and the store as the abstraction behind local/S3 — functional WHYs but the answer doesn't explain why Upload is the single shared media record that all of posts/avatars/themes/email/backup must reference rather than having per-feature storage.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer flags no uncertainty anywhere; all paths and roles are stated as confirmed facts, giving the agent no signal about which connections to verify before acting.

relationship.rationale: The answer states the polymorphic join (UploadReference as 'glue between an upload and posts/themes/etc.'), the concern mechanism (HasUrl for sha1/url resolution), and the storage route (Discourse.store) — concrete connecting paths for the core pieces; avatars, email, and backup are not mapped to their specific association/derive-URL routes.

steps.step: Map the Upload contract and what it is built on

map_quality.rationale: The answer maps the concern (HasUrl at concerns/has_url.rb:3), every association with file:line, variant spawning (optimized_images, optimized_videos), the teardown seam, the full URL/identity surface, and UploadReference — an agent can enumerate exactly what a contract change must preserve with only small follow-up reads.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every piece carries file:line (e.g., upload.rb:27 for optimized_images, upload.rb:35,690 for the video callback, concerns/has_url.rb:3 for HasUrl); the main gap is omitting full paths for app/models/optimized_image.rb and the optimized_video models, but the answer stays actionably concrete throughout.

justification.rationale: Some good WHY appears — access_control_post unscoped 'so deleted posts still resolve — security-critical', HasUrl described as 'the contract other code uses to turn a URL/path back into an Upload' — but the answer does not explain why URL resolution is extracted into a concern (cross-model reuse) or why variants are separate records rather than columns, leaving the structural motivation partially implicit.

uncertainty.rationale: No part of the answer is flagged as unconfirmed; every association, method, and line number is presented as verified fact, so the agent cannot tell which pieces the answerer actually opened vs. inferred.

relationship.rationale: HasUrl's relationship is stated explicitly ('class methods keyed off URL_REGEX… the contract other code uses to turn a URL/path back into an Upload'); variant derivation is traced via 'create_thumbnail! → OptimizedImage.create_for'; UploadReference is described as 'polymorphic :target… bulk reconcile entry used by every non-post dependent'; most bindings have their connecting route, not just a file listing.

steps.step: Trace how an upload is created and processed

map_quality.rationale: The answer traces the full chain in order — UploadCreator:59 → store_upload:259 → inline fan-out (avatar enqueue:276, UserUpload:280, dominant color:214) → OptimizedImage:35 → CookedPostProcessor async → scheduled jobs — with file:line at each layer and inline/async labels throughout. The agent can follow this path to change it safely with only small targeted lookups.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every step carries a concrete file:line (upload_creator.rb:59/132-148/91-117/259/276, base_store.rb:12,124, optimized_image.rb:35/11-24/66-71, upload.rb:690, has_post_upload_references.rb:6) rather than vague layer names; the few job file paths for remote storage jobs lack line numbers but are fully named.

justification.rationale: The answer labels each step inline/async but never explains WHY heavy processing is deferred (to keep the request fast and not block the response thread); the rubric specifically asks for this design rationale, not just the labeling.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer implicitly signals context-dependence ('inline where called', 'external-upload path uses move_existing_stored_upload') but never explicitly flags code paths it did not open or tells the agent to verify a specific step before acting; a blanket 'I didn't confirm the S3Store override' would have raised the score.

relationship.rationale: The chain is assembled as an explicit ordered path (creator → dedup → image processing → build row → store → fan-out → variants → cooking → remote jobs) with each link's connection stated (e.g., store_upload called at upload_creator.rb:259, OptimizedImage.create_for guarded by DistributedMutex, video after_create enqueues :convert_video); the route/order is clear, not just a list of files.

steps.step: Audit every dependent of the Upload contract

map_quality.rationale: The answer covers all eight rubric areas with file:line throughout: post rendering, storage/CDN, jobs, email, backup, theming, user profiles, and plugins. Gaps are limited but real: lib/user_merger.rb (explicitly called out in the rubric under 'user profiles and merge') is absent, lib/email/styles.rb (the secure-image styler) is not named, and lib/site_setting_extension.rb is missing — the answer names app/models/site_setting.rb instead.

specificity.rationale: Almost every entry carries an exact file:line and a one-phrase description of how the upload is used (e.g., 'lib/s3_inventory.rb — reconciles Upload rows against S3 inventory, sets verification_status'), with no 'various helpers'-style collapses; the few serializer lists in section G/I are the only mild groupings.

justification.rationale: Per-entry WHY is consistently present ('destroys orphaned Uploads', 'rewrites upload URLs across local/S3/CDN base URLs', 'strips/embeds secure upload images by sha1 map'), and area grouping itself signals the architectural reason; what is missing is any higher-level explanation of why the dependent set is so scattered (shared URL resolution concern, polymorphic UploadReference surface, multi-store abstraction).

uncertainty.rationale: The answer contains no flag acknowledging what a static pass cannot reach: dependents touched only through dynamic dispatch, concerns that pull behavior in implicitly, or entries that derive a URL/path without naming Upload directly — the agent is left to assume the list is complete rather than being prompted to verify edge paths.

relationship.rationale: Most dependents include their connecting path: association types are named ('belongs_to :gravatar_upload / :custom_upload, nulled in upload.rb:60-64'), concern inclusions are explicit ('include HasPostUploadReferences'), store routes are stated ('Discourse.store.path_for / download!'), and plugin hooks are quoted ('Upload.add_unused_callback, upload.rb:96-118'); the serializer block in section G is the one area that lists files without connecting relations.

steps.step: Trace how upload identity and storage location are guarded

map_quality.rationale: The answer catalogs the guards and resolvers well with file:line throughout — sha1 validation chain, verification_status, BaseStore path resolution, secure URL conversion — but does not trace them per-service (post-processing, email, backup, S3 reconciliation each as a named path); the agent gets the pieces but must still assemble which guards fire in which service path.

specificity.rationale: File:line is present on nearly every item — `upload.rb:86`, `has_url.rb:21-47`, `base_store.rb:124-140`, `secure_upload_endpoint_helpers.rb:17-24` — giving the agent precise insertion points with no vague prose like 'the validation code'.

justification.rationale: A few WHY notes appear (`access_control_post` 'ensures deleted posts still gate access', `perform_download` 'chooses signed vs cdn URL based on secure?') but the core reason — uploads may be secure, missing, or on remote storage, each requiring a different check — is never stated explicitly; it is implied by grouping, not argued.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer flags nothing as unverified or potentially missing; no path is called out as 'assumes the URL resolves without a guard' or 'could not confirm this check exists in the email path', leaving the agent to assume coverage is complete.

relationship.rationale: `has_been_uploaded?` is tied to `has_post_upload_references.rb:123` and `perform_download` is linked to `secure?`, but most guards — `HasUrl#get_from_url`, `verification_status` scopes, `UploadRecovery#ensure_upload!`, `get_path_for_upload` — are listed without stating which downstream effect or service each one gates; the chain from guard to protected operation is largely absent.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: The answer covers all seven rubric areas (post rendering, storage/CDN, jobs, email, backup, theming/profiles, plugins) with a three-tier risk ranking and file:line throughout; theming is the thin spot ('Theme/site-setting/avatar serializers' in #11 lacks specific file:line), and background jobs are implied rather than enumerated, but an agent could start safely from this map.

specificity.rationale: Concrete file:line for the critical and high items (upload.rb:53-64, has_post_upload_references.rb:6,56, base_store.rb:124-140, email/sender.rb:364-412, uploads_restorer.rb:88) with a stated reason per entry; item #11 ('Theme/site-setting/avatar serializers and Discourse.store consumers') is the only grouping that skips specific paths.

justification.rationale: Each tier explains the failure mode, not just the location: #6 chains URL change → orphaned UploadReference rows → 'silent deletion' by clean_up_uploads, #3 explains the fail-open vs fail-closed split on secure file access, #2 names the specific S3 file-leak mechanism; the WHY is present and concrete for every critical and high item.

uncertainty.rationale: The 'Must re-verify' footer and cross-references to 'Step 4 G/H' signal awareness of open edges, but the answer never flags a specific file it could not confirm or a concern/association path it only reached structurally; the agent gets a checklist, not a confidence map.

relationship.rationale: Most entries state the connecting path: #1 names belongs_to FK ownership, #6 explicitly says 'Cooked-HTML URL parsing is pattern-coupled to the URL scheme', #7 states 'Path derived from URL', #13 names the hook API surface; item #11 ('Discourse.store consumers') is the one group that lists files without the relationship, but the majority of high-risk items carry the route.

map_quality.rationale: The answer delivers a complete, actionable map across all required areas: model + shared pieces with method-level line ranges, dependents grouped into 10 named areas, specs/fixtures with explicit file paths, and three new test scenarios spelled out. The JSON block reinforces the prose with structured creation_flow, teardown_seam, and blast_radius_ranked entries — a teammate has everything needed to land the change without re-reading the codebase.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every entry is concrete to method + line (e.g., `upload.rb:53-64 before_destroy`, `email/sender.rb:256-273 secure embed/strip`, `uploads_restorer.rb:57/88`); the few items without line numbers (e.g., `local_store.rb`, `to_s3_migration.rb`) are the exception, not the rule.

justification.rationale: The blast_radius_ranked section explicitly states consequences per entry ('orphaned/leaked FK owners', 'fail-open security risk', 'path/url desync'), and area groupings carry implicit WHY reasoning; however the long prose lists in 'Dependents to review/touch' drop the per-file rationale to just file names, so a reader must infer why `convert_video.rb` or `badge.rb:110` belongs.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer does not flag any part of the map as uncertain or unverified — no 'could not confirm', 'may need checking', or 'indirect path unsure' markers appear anywhere in the prose or JSON, leaving the agent without explicit cues about where to double-check before committing.

relationship.rationale: The JSON block assembles explicit chains: creation_flow names the route from entry to storage to async variants; teardown_seam names each callback and what it nulls; identity_and_url_guards maps each resolver to what it gates. The prose dependent lists are weaker — items like `rake tasks` or `letter_avatar.rb` appear without a stated connecting route — but the structured section covers the high-value paths with explicit relationships.
